title: Caryophyllus racemosus
intro: Caryophyllus racemosus, commonly known as Dillenia racemosa or Dillenia indica, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caricaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Caryophyllus racemosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules for various health applications.
taxonomy: Caryophyllus racemosus is classified in the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This species is further classified as Caryophyllus racemosus within the genus Caryophyllus. The genus Caryophyllus is a part of the order Caryophyllales. Caryophyllus racemosus is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia.

properties: Caryophyllus racemosus, also known as the pink winter-flowering caryophyllus, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. constituents: Caryophyllus racemosus, also known as Dianthus plumarius, contains various active constituents. These include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant's essential oil, which is extracted from its flowers, is rich in geraniol, linalool, and beta-caryophyllene. These compounds are responsible for the plant's fragrance and have potential therapeutic applications. The exact composition of Caryophyllus racemosus' active constituents may vary depending on the specific plant source and extraction methods used.
